findings.title: Share-token clone copies day-plan text even when share_show_plans=0, bypassing the public-page plan gate
findings.rootCause: `fetch_share_payload` (public.py:591-622) gates the plan SELECT and the per-day `plan`/`tip` attachment on `show_plans = bool(row['share_show_plans'])` — so a share with `share_show_plans=0` (DEFAULT 0 in database.py:268; share modal defaults `showPlans:false`, share.ts:148) never sends plan text to the viewer. However the clone path does NOT honour that flag: `clone_trip_from_share_token` (trips.py:1515) calls `_clone_trip_record(cursor, row['id'], user_id)` with no privacy argument, and `_clone_trip_record` (trips.py:1384-1425) unconditionally SELECTs `morning, afternoon, evening, tip` and INSERTs them into the clone's trip_days. The clone copies the source rows directly from the DB, ignoring the share_show_plans gate that governs what the recipient was actually shown.

findings.title: Clone copies the owner's full markedPlaces wishlist, which the share page and public-trip read both strip from non-members
findings.rootCause: `_clone_trip_attempt` (trips.py:1309) copies `src['marked_places_json']` directly into the clone with the comment "copied verbatim (the user's wishlist of places is half the value of cloning)". This deliberate copy contradicts the share/public-trip privacy contract: both read surfaces treat markedPlaces as owner-private. The clone is the only surface that hands the wishlist to a non-member, and it does so unconditionally for both clone entry points.

findings.title: Cloned trip's cover image breaks when the source owner revokes the share or deletes the trip
findings.rootCause: `_clone_trip_attempt` copies the SOURCE owner's `cover_url` verbatim (trips.py:1319), e.g. `/static/uploads/<ownerA>/abc.jpg`. The clone row is private: `is_archived=0, is_public=0`, no share_token. The serve_upload gate (main.py:1294) can't authorize the cloner: the owner-dir fast-path fails (file is in ownerA's dir, not the cloner's, main.py:1326), and the member-join requires BOTH the caller and the file's owner to be accepted members of a trip referencing the file (main.py:1364-1378) — but ownerA is not a member of the clone, and the cloner is not a member of the source. The only thing keeping the cover visible is the anonymous public-cover fallback (main.py:1448-1456), which matches `cover_url` only on trips where `is_public=1 OR share_token IS NOT NULL`. That row is the SOURCE trip. So the clone's cover lives or dies by the source's share/public state: revoke the share link (revoke_share_link sets share_token=NULL, trips.py:1233) or unpublish, and the clone's cover stops resolving. Delete the source trip and delete_upload_files removes the physical file (trips.py:347/455), 404ing it forever.
